ground_truth.title: Costa Rica--Guided Tour or Car Rental?
ground_truth.author: linahx
ground_truth.main_content:
Forum discussion about whether to take a guided tour or rent a car for a first trip to Costa Rica. Original poster linahx asks for advice, expressing concerns about bad roads, unmarked roads, and theft targeting rental cars. Multiple forum members provide their experiences and recommendations.

Mochi shares that they drove in Costa Rica twice and found the roads surprisingly good, recommending a 4WD for first visit but a regular sedan worked fine on their second trip. They note that while road signs are not well posted, careful map reading helps navigate.

airwayvz suggests the decision depends on personal preferences and comfort level with driving. They recommend renting a car for the adventure of exploring independently, getting lost, and finding unexpected places. However, if the idea of driving causes anxiety that would affect vacation enjoyment, they advise against it. They also mention hiring private drivers as an alternative to guided tours.

sandyc recommends considering custom tours with transfers rather than fully guided tours, and mentions contacting a specific travel agent in Costa Rica for pricing.

airwayvz recommends Pat Hewitt from Costa Rica Travel Exchange (www.lovecostarica.com) for helping plan a first trip.

shillmac suggests using Interbus for transfers (www.interbusonline.com). They also advocate for driving, citing freedom and adventure benefits. They note most roads are in decent condition despite horror stories.

Dyer recommends contacting Leo Rodriguez for advice on driving conditions in specific areas.

swim strongly recommends 4WD for visiting coastal areas or flexible exploration, based on three visits to Costa Rica.
with: Hi All!! We are planning our first trip to CR and can't decide which is best: guided tour or independent travel with a rental car.
I would vote for renting a car if you want sense of adventure. My husband and I have been to Costa Rica twice and we drove both times.
The main roads are maybe OK for sedan, but if you want to go to the coast or just enjoy to go where you want, I very strongly recommend 4WD.

This answer to this question depends on how you feel about both options really, as there is no 'best'. I would much rather rent a car, drive myself, maybe get lost and find some cool new & unexpected places.

Most roads are in decent condition. There are a very few roads that are awful. You may well end up on one of them depending on your itinerary, but I think it is safe to say that MOST of the roads you will be on will surprise you--especially after listening to all the horror stories!
